message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first wait_for_shutdown poll (tick 289)

Migrates the `wait_for_shutdown` helper (main.rs L16371) to use
`sleep_with_cx` with cancel-forwarding:

- `runtime_compat::sleep(Duration::from_millis(250))` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&wait_cx, Duration::from_millis(250))` with Err-handling
  that returns early (treating cx-cancel as shutdown-equivalent).

This is a 250ms-poll shutdown-flag watcher used at various CLI teardown
points. Previously the poll loop was cx-insensitive — a cx-cancel at
the outer async task couldn't propagate here. Now cx-cancel short-
circuits the wait: `sleep_with_cx` returning Err → return → caller
observes the wait ended (semantically equivalent to the shutdown_flag
firing).

Pattern mirrors ticks 282/283/285: cx-cancel flows into existing
shutdown-semantics paths without requiring signature changes.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 103 (187-289).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 188 sites (187 before + 1).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
